name: Competitor Watch
description: Monitors competitor websites, pricing pages, job listings, and social accounts for meaningful changes — then sends you a plain-English summary of what changed and why it matters.
author: ProwlrBot Team

before_after.before: Manually checking competitor websites every few days, missing pricing changes, and finding out about new product launches from your customers instead of ahead of time
before_after.after: Instant alerts when competitors change pricing, launch features, post jobs, or update messaging — with a weekly digest summarizing the competitive landscape

user_stories.quote: A competitor dropped their prices by 20% on a Tuesday. I found out within the hour and adjusted our positioning before we lost a single deal.
user_stories.name: Nathan B.

user_stories.quote: I'm a freelance web designer and I watch 5 agencies in my area. When one of them updated their portfolio, I saw what kind of clients they're going after and pivoted my outreach.
user_stories.name: Morgan C.

user_stories.quote: Their job postings told me they were hiring 3 data engineers before they announced their analytics product. Gave us a 2-month head start on our response.
user_stories.name: Hiroshi T.

setup_steps.instruction: Add competitor URLs you want to monitor — homepages, pricing pages, blog, careers page, etc.
Set monitoring frequency: real-time (checks every hour), daily, or weekly.
Choose what types of changes to track — pricing, new features, job postings, messaging changes, or all.
Pick your alert channel (email, Slack, Discord) and choose between instant alerts and weekly digests.
Optionally add competitor social accounts (Twitter/X, LinkedIn) for broader coverage.
